questions.question: Which of the following breeding methods involves mating of animals within the same breed, but having no common ancestors on either side of their pedigree up to 4-6 generations?
The largest gland in the human body is: (Exam form 506)
questions.solution: By definition, Liver performs this role.

questions.question: In the process of producing a Mule, which of the following matings occurs?
Which of the following is a classic characteristic of an improved breed of poultry like the White Leghorn?
What is the economic significance of 'Propolis' sourced from bee hives?

options.A: It is a resinous mixture used for its medicinal and structural properties
options.B: It is the primary sugar source used in confectionery
options.C: It is used exclusively to feed the queen bee larva
options.D: It acts as a pheromone to attract drones to the hive

questions.question: Artificial Insemination requires careful handling of semen. If the semen is frozen for long-term storage, what temperature and medium are typically used?
Which component of poultry feed is essential to ensure a strong eggshell for lying hens (layers)?
Which indigenous breed of cow is prominently known in India for its hardiness and milk yield?
Which of the following describes 'Heterosis' in animal breeding?

options.A: The superior performance of hybrid progeny over both parental strains
options.B: The decline in fertility due to continuous inbreeding
options.C: The emergence of entirely new species from two distinct genera
options.D: The uniform characteristics observed in a pure-line

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding honeybees. Which one is true?

options.A: The queen bee is fertile, while worker bees are sterile females.
options.B: The drone bee is a sterile male.
options.C: Worker bees are responsible only for reproduction.
options.D: The queen bee gathers nectar and produces wax.

questions.question: Which of the following practices does NOT fall under 'Animal Husbandry'?
What is the primary factor driving the success of a dairy farm's overall milk production?

options.A: The genetic quality and breed of the cattle
options.B: The geographical location of the farm
options.D: The temperature of the milking parlor
